title: Fixing TF to < 2.16
body:
### Description
<!--- Describe your changes in detail -->
<!--- Why is this change required? What problem does it solve? -->


### Related Issues
<!--- If it fixes an open issue, please link to the issue here. -->
#2073

### References
<!--- References would be helpful to understand the changes. -->
<!--- References can be books, links, etc. -->


### Checklist:
<!--- Go over all the following points, and put an `x` in all the boxes that apply. -->
<!--- If you're unsure about any of these, don't hesitate to ask. We're here to help! -->
- [ ] I have followed the [contribution guidelines](CONTRIBUTING.md) and code style for this project.
- [ ] I have added tests covering my contributions.
- [ ] I have updated the documentation accordingly.
- [ ] I have [signed the commits](https://github.com/recommenders-team/recommenders/wiki/How-to-sign-commits), e.g. `git commit -s -m "your commit message"`. 
- [ ] This PR is being made to `staging branch` AND NOT TO `main branch`.

files_modified.patch:
@@ -62,18 +62,22 @@ def score(
 
     # score the pytorch model
     x = torch.column_stack((u, m))
+
     if torch.cuda.is_available():
         x = x.to("cuda")
         learner.model = learner.model.to("cuda")
+
     pred = learner.model.forward(x).detach().cpu().numpy()
     scores = pd.DataFrame(
         {user_col: test_df[user_col], item_col: test_df[item_col], prediction_col: pred}
     )
     scores = scores.sort_values([user_col, prediction_col], ascending=[True, False])
+
     if top_k is not None:
         top_scores = scores.groupby(user_col).head(top_k).reset_index(drop=True)
     else:
         top_scores = scores
+
     return top_scores

@@ -53,7 +53,7 @@
     "gpu": [
         "fastai>=2.7.11,<3",
         "nvidia-ml-py>=11.525.84",
-        "tensorflow>=2.8.4,!=2.9.0.*,!=2.9.1,!=2.9.2,!=2.10.0.*,<=2.15.0",
+        "tensorflow>=2.8.4,!=2.9.0.*,!=2.9.1,!=2.9.2,!=2.10.0.*,<2.16",  # Fixed TF due to constant security problems and breaking changes #2073
         "tf-slim>=1.1.0",  # No python_requires in its setup.py
         "torch>=2.0.1,<3",
     ],

@@ -247,7 +247,9 @@ def test_wide_deep_functional(
             os.path.join("tests", "resources", "deeprec", "slirec"),
             10,
             400,
-            {"auc": 0.7183},  # Don't do logloss check as SLi-Rec uses ranking loss, not a point-wise loss
+            {
+                "auc": 0.7183
+            },  # Don't do logloss check as SLi-Rec uses ranking loss, not a point-wise loss
             42,
         )
     ],
@@ -278,7 +280,7 @@ def test_slirec_quickstart_functional(
     results = read_notebook(output_notebook)
 
     assert results["auc"] == pytest.approx(expected_values["auc"], rel=TOL, abs=ABS_TOL)
-    
+
 
 @pytest.mark.gpu
 @pytest.mark.notebooks
@@ -567,7 +569,7 @@ def test_dkn_quickstart_functional(notebooks, output_notebook, kernel_name):
         notebook_path,
         output_notebook,
         kernel_name=kernel_name,
-        parameters=dict(EPOCHS=5, BATCH_SIZE=500),
+        parameters=dict(EPOCHS=5, BATCH_SIZE=200),
     )
     results = read_notebook(output_notebook)

steps.operation: Update the FastAI scoring path to move both input tensors and the model onto CUDA when available before running the forward pass
steps.reason: Prevent device-mismatch runtime errors and ensure GPU-enabled environments actually execute inference on the GPU by aligning model and input tensor devices.
steps.side_effects: Inference will consume GPU memory when CUDA is available; this may change performance characteristics and can surface new CUDA-related issues (e.g., out-of-memory) in environments where the model previously ran on CPU.

steps.operation: Pin TensorFlow GPU extra to a version range below 2.16 to avoid known breakages/security-driven changes
steps.reason: Stabilize dependency resolution for GPU installations by preventing installation of TensorFlow 2.16+ versions that introduce breaking changes and recurring security-related forced upgrades (per issue #2073).
steps.side_effects: Users attempting to install with newer TensorFlow versions will be constrained/downgraded; downstream packages requiring TF>=2.16 may become incompatible with this extra.

steps.operation: Adjust GPU notebook functional test parameters (reduce DKN batch size; minor formatting cleanup) to improve reliability under constrained GPU memory
steps.reason: Reduce the likelihood of CUDA out-of-memory failures in CI/functional runs by lowering the DKN notebook batch size from 500 to 200 while keeping the test intent intact.
steps.side_effects: Notebook execution time may increase due to smaller batches; training dynamics can shift slightly, which could affect metric stability if future assertions are tightened.

step_plan.summary: This PR pins the TensorFlow GPU dependency to <2.16 for stability, improves FastAI GPU inference by moving both model and inputs to CUDA, and makes GPU notebook functional tests more reliable by reducing the DKN batch size.
